tactical_shape: Used Brian Brobbey as a focal point up front
Attacked strongly down the flanks, with Denzel Dumfries repeatedly crossing from the right
Cody Gakpo and Crysencio Summerville operated in advanced wide roles and combined effectively
Took advantage when Sweden loosened into a back four after the first hydration break
standout_players: Brian Brobbey — scored twice in the opening 17 minutes and was impossible to handle; brought strength, touch and finishing
Cody Gakpo — scored twice and was heavily involved in the first goal
Denzel Dumfries — supplied several marvellous, snaking crosses from the right
Crysencio Summerville — sparkled after half-time, then scored late
Bart Verbruggen — made smart saves when Sweden briefly threatened
Ronald Koeman — his selection of Brobbey was vindicated
home_read.momentum: A statement win that should do wonders for morale; the team looks improved and capable of hurting opponents with its attacking quality.

tactical_shape: Started in a shape that left Viktor Gyökeres and Alexander Isak isolated early on
Switched to a back four after the first-half hydration break, which loosened their setup
Were repeatedly exposed and torn apart down the flanks
Sent balls across the box from both flanks after the break but lacked a killer touch
standout_players: Viktor Gyökeres — tested Bart Verbruggen multiple times
Alexander Isak — also tested the keeper but was criticised for needless squandering of possession
Yasin Ayari — forced a smart save and had a shot go narrowly over
Anthony Elanga — scored after being played through by Isak
away_read.momentum: A damaging defeat that left them dizzy and vulnerable, with their World Cup hopes needing a quick response against Japan.
